data.title: Add hyperfine performance test asserting ticket show/list <0.15s (DD4 of 564c-e391)
data.description:
## Task

Implement the missing verification for Done Definition 4 of story 564c-e391: 'read-path operations (ticket show, ticket list) complete in <0.15s per call on Linux reference hardware — verified via hyperfine against the A0 baseline.'

## What to implement

Create tests/scripts/test-ticket-perf.sh that:
1. Requires hyperfine; skips gracefully (exit 0 with a note) if unavailable — so CI and local envs without hyperfine don't fail
2. Sets up an isolated temp ticket environment (TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR)
3. Creates a test ticket
4. Runs: hyperfine --warmup 3 --runs 10 --export-json /tmp/bench-show.json '.claude/scripts/dso ticket show <id>'
5. Runs: hyperfine --warmup 3 --runs 10 --export-json /tmp/bench-list.json '.claude/scripts/dso ticket list'
6. Parses the JSON and asserts mean < 0.15 for both ops
7. Also reads tests/fixtures/ticket-cli-baseline.json (if present) and prints a comparison note (not a hard assertion — the baseline is macOS arm64, not Linux)

The test should emit PASS/FAIL lines per the repo's bash test convention.

## Done definition
- tests/scripts/test-ticket-perf.sh exists and is executable
- Running it with hyperfine available produces PASS output for both show and list ops
- The test skips gracefully (not fails) when hyperfine is unavailable
- .test-index updated to map plugins/dso/scripts/ticket to this test
